tasks.prompt: As of the benchmark reference time, which Atlas sync API is current?
tasks.query: current Atlas sync GraphQL v2 API
tasks.expected_answer: Use the GraphQL v2 Atlas sync API.

evidence.title: Deprecated Atlas REST sync API
evidence.content: The old Atlas sync REST v1 endpoint was retired before the reference time and must not be treated as current.

evidence.title: Current Atlas GraphQL v2 sync API
evidence.content: Use GraphQL v2 for current Atlas sync API calls after the reference-time migration.

tasks.prompt: Should the old webpack-only deploy constraint still be followed?
tasks.query: Vite deployment superseded webpack deploy constraint
tasks.expected_answer: No. The Vite deployment decision superseded the old webpack-only constraint.

evidence.title: Old webpack-only deployment constraint
evidence.content: The app used to require webpack-only deploy steps, but this deployment memory is stale.

evidence.title: Vite deployment supersedes webpack
evidence.content: The current deployment path uses Vite; do not follow the stale webpack-only deployment constraint.

tasks.prompt: When storage memories conflict, which storage rule should be followed?
tasks.expected_answer: Follow the SQLCipher encrypted storage rule and reject the stale plaintext-memory claim.

evidence.title: Plaintext memory store claim
evidence.content: A stale storage note claimed private memory could be stored in plaintext.

evidence.title: SQLCipher private memory storage
evidence.content: Private memory storage must use SQLCipher encryption at rest; this resolves the plaintext conflict.

tasks.prompt: Which workstream identity should continue after the queue runner rename?
tasks.query: queue runner rename workstream continuity capture ledger drain
tasks.expected_answer: Continue the capture-ledger drain workstream under the queue-runner rename.

evidence.title: Capture-ledger drain queue-runner rename
evidence.content: The queue-runner rename is the same capture-ledger drain workstream; continue the existing workstream identity after the rename.

tasks.prompt: What was the root cause of the duplicate startup summary bug?
tasks.query: duplicate startup summary root cause summarize cooldown race
tasks.expected_answer: The root cause was a summarize cooldown race that allowed duplicate startup summaries.

evidence.title: Duplicate startup summary root cause
evidence.content: The duplicate startup summary bug was caused by a summarize cooldown race; the fix serialized the cooldown claim.

tasks.prompt: Can durable memory capture depend on agents voluntarily calling save_memory?
tasks.query: automatic capture primary manual save_memory supplemental
tasks.expected_answer: No. Automatic hook capture is primary; manual save_memory is only supplemental.

evidence.title: Automatic capture primary architecture
evidence.content: Durable memory capture must not depend on agents voluntarily calling save_memory; automatic hook capture is the primary path and manual save_memory is supplemental.

tasks.prompt: Which file anchors support the API handler split decision?
tasks.query: API handler split file anchors handlers routes server
tasks.expected_answer: The split is anchored in src/api/handlers.rs and src/api/server.rs.

evidence.title: API handler split file anchors
evidence.content: The API handler split decision is anchored by src/api/handlers.rs and src/api/server.rs.

tasks.prompt: Which user-context preference is relevant to a PR handoff summary?
tasks.query: user preference concise evidence backed PR handoff Chinese
tasks.expected_answer: Use concise, evidence-backed Chinese for the PR handoff.

evidence.title: Concise evidence-backed Chinese handoffs
evidence.content: For PR handoffs, the user prefers concise, evidence-backed Chinese with exact PR and verification status.

evidence.title: Marketing launch copy style
evidence.content: Marketing launch copy can use splashy English phrases; this is not relevant to engineering PR handoff summaries.
